what_it_does: Defines a 24 by 24 SVG info-circle icon using a stroked outer circle, a vertical info stem, and a filled dot.
observations: The SVG uses viewBox 0 0 24 24 with explicit width and height of 24.
The icon has fill="none" at the root and stroke="currentColor" for inherited color.
Stroke styling uses width 1.5 with round caps and round joins.
The outer circle is centered at 12,12 with radius 9.
The info stem is a path from y=11 to y=16.5 at x=12.
The info dot is a small filled circle at 12,8 with stroke disabled.
standouts: Combines stroked geometry with a filled dot for the info mark.
Uses currentColor so the icon can inherit text or component color.
conventions: Follows common line-icon SVG conventions: 24px canvas, no root fill, currentColor stroke, round line caps and joins.
Uses simple primitive SVG shapes without symbols, defs, classes, or embedded styling.
Keeps the asset inline and minified on one line.
concerns: No standalone accessibility metadata such as title, role, or aria attributes is present.
notes: A compact reusable SVG asset for an information icon in a line-icon style.
questions: Is accessibility labeling supplied by the consuming component?
Is the one-line SVG format intentional for asset delivery or generated output?
purpose_vs_actual: The filename and path indicate an info-circle icon, and the SVG content renders that icon.
intention_signals: It appears intended to provide a themeable information icon that can be dropped into UI components without fixed color styling.
novelty: The dot is rendered as a filled circle while the surrounding icon remains stroke-based.
contribution: Contributes a themeable information-symbol asset with clear circular containment and simple SVG geometry that a fused system would keep as an icon primitive.
summary_for_embedding: A 24x24 currentColor SVG info-circle icon made from a stroked outer circle, stroked vertical info stem, and filled dot.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for supplying a reusable design-system information icon that inherits color from its rendering context.
